turns.user_message: I keep catching myself apologizing for things that aren't even my fault, and it has been happening for months now. It feels like I'm constantly walking on eggshells around my own thoughts, waiting for the other shoe to drop. Whenever a small mistake slips through, I immediately assume everyone is secretly judging me for it. I just wish I could stop carrying this invisible weight around without knowing exactly where it came from. It's exhausting to feel so responsible for every little hiccup in the room.

I've started realizing that this habit isn't just a passing phase, but a heavy pattern I can't seem to shake. Every time I try to step up, I end up overthinking my every move until I freeze up completely. I replay conversations in my head for days, convinced I said the wrong thing or ruined the mood. The guilt piles up so fast that I barely have room to breathe before the next wave hits. I hate how quickly I turn minor slip-ups into proof that I'm fundamentally flawed.

I finally understand that this cycle has completely taken over how I see myself, and it feels impossible to break free. I catch myself shrinking away from opportunities because I already know I'm going to disappoint someone eventually. The shame sits so deep in my chest that it colors everything I do, making even simple choices feel like minefields. I keep waiting for the day I finally get it right, but the evidence just keeps stacking against me. It's hard to keep pretending I'm fine when I feel like a walking mistake.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something they noticed about themselves. This has been going on for a while.
